response.justification: While Bogdan demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals, distributed-systems expertise, and proven mentorship capabilities, he represents a critical domain mismatch. His entire recent career (2015–present, 11 years) has focused on web development (TypeScript, Node.js, React), with C++ experience limited to a 6-month role at National Instruments ending in 2015—over a decade ago. The position requires deep hands-on C++, STL, and industrial expertise (MQTT, IEC 61850, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 62443 security standards), none of which appear in his background. While his general engineering caliber is high, the 10-year C++ gap and complete absence of industrial automation, IoT, or secure development lifecycle experience create unacceptable ramp-up risk for a senior role that must deliver immediately.
strengths: Expert in high-performance, large-scale distributed systems (handled millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby with async I/O, fibers, and concurrent architectures)
Exceptional software craftsmanship foundations: TDD, design patterns, architecture, mentorship, and strong ownership mindset demonstrated across roles
Proven ability to learn and excel across diverse technology stacks and domains (web, mobile, desktop, embedded, DevOps infrastructure)
concerns: C++ expertise effectively atrophied; no hands-on C++ development for 10+ years, with entire recent career (TypeScript, Node.js) in different paradigm
Zero background in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, IEC 104, OPC UA) central to the role
No evidence of security expertise: absent Secure Development Lifecycle, IEC 62443 standards, secure coding practices, or industrial cybersecurity knowledge explicitly required

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IoT background
